summary: Matched more of Kirby's copied Fox/Falco neutral-special code in src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialfox.c, most notably implementing ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDF30 instead of leaving it as an unmatched stub and preserving ftKb_SpecialNFx_CreateBlasterShot as a wrapper. The change added item/projectile creation logic for Fox and Falco hats, moved blaster item-id selection inline into the ground and air start functions, added needed includes for ft_0877 and itfoxlaser, and promoted several ftKb_Init externs into ftKb_Init.h. Review centered on whether the initial goto-based match was too fake; the author revised to a goto-less conditional form. Review also pushed back against replacing proper OSReport/HSD_ASSERT usage with raw string/assert symbol calls.
what_changed: Implemented ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDF30 in ftkirbyspecialfox.c, replacing a placeholder unmatched marker and moving the previous direct call into ftKb_SpecialNFx_CreateBlasterShot.

ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDF30 now checks fp->cmd_vars[2], computes the blaster shot spawn position from FtPart_R3rdNa via lb_8000B1CC when possible, clears sp2C.z, and dispatches Fox/Falco-specific projectile creation.
Fox hat path uses da->specialn_fx_launch_angle, specialn_fx_launch_speed, specialn_fx_blaster_projectile_id, it_8029C6A4, it_802AE1D0, and ftKb_Init_804D3DB8 indexed by facing direction for SFX.
Falco hat path uses da->specialn_fc_launch_angle, specialn_fc_launch_speed, specialn_fc_blaster_projectile_id, it_8029C6A4, it_802AE1D0, and ftKb_Init_804D3DC0 indexed by facing direction for SFX.
Removed inline helper ftKbGetBlasterId and duplicated the Fox/Falco blaster item-id switch at the call sites in ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE100 and ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE240 to satisfy matching constraints.
Added includes for ft/ft_0877.h and it/items/itfoxlaser.h.

Added extern declarations for ftKb_Init_804D3DB8, ftKb_Init_804D3DC0, ftKb_Init_804D9478, and ftKb_Init_804D9480 to ftKb_Init.h, and removed local extern declarations for the two float symbols from ftkirbyspecialfox.c.
smart_moves: Kept ftKb_SpecialNFx_CreateBlasterShot as a small wrapper around ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDF30, preserving the existing named entry point while matching the underlying function.

Used explicit Fox/Falco switch cases tied to fp->fv.kb.hat.kind so copied Kirby blaster behavior can select correct launch parameters, projectile ids, item ids, and SFX ids from ftKb_DatAttrs and ftKb_Init data.
Accepted reviewer feedback and replaced the initial goto-based structure with a cleaner goto-less conditional that still matched, reducing the obvious fake-match smell.
Moved global data externs from a C file into ftKb_Init.h, making the symbols reusable and easier to locate for future Kirby work.
Preserved PAD_STACK only as a matching tactic; review discussion treats it as tolerated technical debt rather than desirable source style.
decomp_lessons: A match that uses a goto in a place where original source likely did not should be considered suspicious; if an equivalent structured conditional also matches, prefer the structured version.

Reviewer tolerance for 'fake matches' is contextual: PAD_STACK is widely accepted as temporary project-wide cleanup debt, while contrived gotos, M2C_FIELD-style constructs, or comma-assignment tricks invite scrutiny.
When a helper function prevents matching, inlining its switch logic at multiple call sites may be an acceptable match tactic, but it should be recognized as less clean source reconstruction.

Do not regress readable runtime diagnostics just to expose raw data symbols: reviewers preferred keeping proper OSReport("...") and HSD_ASSERT usage over raw OSReport(symbol) and __assert(file,line,msg) forms.
For copied-character special moves, Kirby hat kind often gates both behavior and data-field selection; matching work should check both FTKIND_FOX and FTKIND_FALCO paths explicitly.

If review questions whether a match is fake and there is no clean solution, a decomp.me scratch or explanatory comment may be useful, but this PR shows reviewers may prefer simply landing a cleaner structured match without labeling obvious temporary weirdness.
naming_conventions: Kirby copied Fox special functions use the ftKb_SpecialNFx_* prefix for Fox/Falco blaster-related neutral special logic.
The wrapper name ftKb_SpecialNFx_CreateBlasterShot is preserved as a descriptive alias while the matched address-style implementation remains ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDF30.
Kirby init/global symbols follow ftKb_Init_80XXXXXX address-style names until better semantic names are available.

Kirby copied-move attribute fields distinguish Fox and Falco with fx/fc infixes, e.g. specialn_fx_launch_angle versus specialn_fc_launch_angle and specialn_fx_blaster_item_id versus specialn_fc_blaster_item_id.
Hat kind comparisons use FTKIND_FOX and FTKIND_FALCO to select behavior for copied specials.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(4) in ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDF30 to satisfy stack/layout matching.
Used f64 launchAngle for angle math involving M_PI and dat attribute launch angles.
Indexed two-element SFX id arrays with the boolean expression -1 == fp->facing_dir to select direction-specific sound ids.
Set fallback Vec3 components with chained assignments sp2C.x = sp2C.y = sp2C.z = 0.0f, then explicitly forced sp2C.z = 0 after the position calculation path.
Replaced an initial goto/label match shape with a structured if/else using assignment inside the condition: if ((gobj != NULL) && (fp2 = GET_FIGHTER(gobj)) != NULL).
Removed an inline helper and repeated switch-based blaster id selection because the factored helper did not match according to the author.

review_feedback.author: ThePlayerRolo
review_feedback.feedback: Called the initial goto-based ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDF30 implementation a likely fake match and suggested including a decomp.me scratch or comment if it could not be solved.

review_feedback.author: lukechampine
review_feedback.feedback: Asked what qualifies as a fake match, noting that M2C_FIELD and comma assignments feel clearly fake while PAD_STACK is tolerated; proposed a cleaner goto-less alternative and said fp2 suggested an inlined function but factoring it out did not restore the match.

review_feedback.author: ThePlayerRolo
review_feedback.feedback: Clarified that the goto looked fake because the original code probably did not use a goto; PAD_STACK was considered a broader project cleanup issue not worth flagging everywhere.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.feedback: Argued that adding a 'fake match' comment would not add much because the goto issue was obvious, and preferred landing the cleaner goto-less option as an improvement over the previous unmatched code.

review_feedback.author: lukechampine
review_feedback.feedback: Pushed the goto-less version in response to the discussion.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Requested not to undo proper OSReport/HSD_ASSERT usage; same feedback applied to multiple hunks where raw string/assert symbols were introduced.

follow_up_queries: Check the final merged ftkirbyspecialfox.c to confirm whether OSReport/HSD_ASSERT usage was restored after PsiLupan's review comments.
Search for other Kirby copied special files that duplicate helper switch logic for hat.kind and may need a shared cleanup after matching.
Look for remaining PAD_STACK and goto-like fake-match constructs in ftKirby files that could be cleaned after more surrounding functions are matched.
Investigate whether the fp2 pattern in ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDF30 corresponds to an inlined helper for joint-position lookup.
